stories.headline: Qatari mediators shuttle to Tehran to finalize US-Iran 'Islamabad' accord as Trump's Sunday Geneva signing slips
stories.summary: Qatari negotiators traveled to Tehran early June 14 to finalize the Pakistan-mediated 'Islamabad Declaration,' which would reopen the Strait of Hormuz, lift the US naval blockade, release about $25bn in frozen Iranian funds and waive oil sanctions during a 60-day window. Trump said VP Vance and Iranian Speaker Qalibaf would sign Sunday in Geneva, but Iran's foreign ministry said no memorandum would be signed Sunday, suggesting a remote signing 'in the coming days.'

perspectives.keyFraming: Wire desks stress the contradiction: Trump says imminent signing, Iran says the text is not finalized — but mediators are now shuttling to close it.
perspectives.summary: NBC News and AP reported Qatari negotiators flew to Tehran early June 14 to finalize the deal, with both sides saying it could be signed within days. Trump's claim of a Sunday signing directly contradicted Iran's foreign ministry, which said no MoU would be signed Sunday and that demining and a transit system for Hormuz still had to be arranged; Pakistan said mediators had reached a final agreed text.

sources.title: Qatari negotiators fly to Tehran in a push to finalize U.S.-Iran deal
Qatari mediators travel to Tehran for final touches on a possible deal to end war

perspectives.keyFraming: Trump frames the accord as a personal diplomatic triumph ending the war and reopening Hormuz.
perspectives.summary: NPR and the Washington Times carried Trump's claim that the signing was set for Sunday in Geneva and that the Strait of Hormuz would reopen the moment the deal is signed, with demining to be discussed with allies at the G7. Iran disputed the timing, but Trump insisted the war was effectively settled.

sources.title: Trump says deal to end Iran war will be signed Sunday, as Iran disagrees on timing
Trump says Iran peace deal to be signed Sunday, but Iran disagrees

perspectives.keyFraming: Pakistani coverage centers Islamabad's mediation and names the accord for Pakistan.
perspectives.summary: Pakistan Observer reported both sides had agreed the final text of the 'Islamabad Declaration,' crediting Pakistani mediation and saying a signing was likely in Geneva near the G7, while noting Tehran's denial that it would be signed Sunday and pointing to a possible electronic signing in the days after.

sources.title: 'Islamabad Declaration' nears final stage, Iran denies Sunday signing

stories.headline: G7 opens at Evian on June 15 with the Iran war and Ukraine front and center; Trump-Macron Versailles dinner set, Zelensky to attend
stories.summary: The G7 summit runs June 15-17 at Evian in the French Alps, with the Iran war and Ukraine dominating. Trump plans a busy schedule of bilaterals — including with Egypt, Qatar and the UAE — on winding down the Iran war and demining the Strait of Hormuz, and will dine with Macron at Versailles. Zelensky will join a working session to press for new Russia sanctions, more air defense and use of frozen Russian assets; no formal Trump-Zelensky meeting is scheduled, though sideline contact is possible.

perspectives.keyFraming: Agenda-setting coverage: Iran and Ukraine dominate, with Trump's bilateral blitz and a Versailles dinner.
perspectives.summary: AP reporting detailed the Evian summit, the Versailles dinner with Macron and Trump's one-on-ones with Egypt, Qatar and the UAE on the Iran war and Hormuz demining, alongside a Ukraine session where allies press for tougher Russia measures. Courthouse News framed the summit as G7 allies trying to bridge divides with Trump.

sources.title: G7 allies seek to bridge divide with Trump at France summit
Trump and Macron Will Meet Over Dinner at Versailles Palace After G7 Summit in France

perspectives.keyFraming: Iran and Ukraine loom over the summit as France hopes Trump stays for the full duration.
perspectives.summary: The Times of Israel reported that the Iran war and Ukraine overshadow the G7, with France anxious that Trump remain for the whole summit and Macron seeking to 'restore the rapprochement of positions' among G7 states on aid to Kyiv, peace talks and security guarantees.

sources.title: Iran and Ukraine loom over G7, as France hopes Trump will at least stay for duration

stories.headline: Israel strikes Hezbollah command center in Beirut's southern suburbs on June 14 after rockets hit northern Israel
stories.summary: Israel said it struck a Hezbollah command center in Beirut's southern suburbs (Dahiyeh) on Sunday June 14 after the group fired three projectiles toward northern Israel, calling it a 'blatant ceasefire violation.' Lebanon's civil defense reported at least three killed and seven wounded, after the Israeli army issued forced-displacement orders for Nabatieh and some 29 towns in the south. The escalation intersects with the US-Iran track.

perspectives.keyFraming: The ceasefire exists on paper but is breached repeatedly along the Lebanon front, now with strikes on Beirut.
perspectives.summary: NBC News reported Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs on June 14, killing three and wounding others, after the Israeli military said Hezbollah fired three projectiles toward northern Israel. The exchanges follow a conditional June 4 ceasefire that Hezbollah rejected and earlier strikes on the Dahiyeh.

sources.title: Israel continues strikes in Beirut's southern suburbs, killing 3
Israel issues forced displacement orders for 29 towns in southern Lebanon

perspectives.keyFraming: Israeli officials frame the strikes as retaliation for Hezbollah rocket fire that violates the truce.
perspectives.summary: Times of Israel's live coverage relayed the Israeli military's account that it struck a Hezbollah command center in Beirut after three drones or projectiles hit northern Israel, arguing the group's refusal to accept the ceasefire terms and its continued fire left it exposed.

sources.title: IDF strikes Hezbollah command center in Beirut after 3 drones hit northern Israel

stories.headline: Gaza death toll passes 73,000 as Israeli strikes continue despite ceasefire; Cairo talks press on amid 'yellow line' dispute
stories.summary: Gaza's Health Ministry said the Palestinian death toll surpassed 73,000 as Israeli operations continued June 14. Mediators in Cairo pressed talks to move past the ceasefire's first phase; Hamas accused Israel of expanding the 'yellow line' westward to derail negotiations, while saying acceptable approaches were reached on some disputed issues. No deal yet on a further Israeli withdrawal.

perspectives.keyFraming: Israel's continued strikes and 'yellow line' creep are eroding a paper ceasefire and threatening the Cairo talks.
perspectives.summary: The Express Tribune reported Hamas accusing Israel of moving the yellow line westward to derail negotiations as the death toll climbed past 73,000, framing the strikes as undermining the US-brokered truce.

sources.title: Israel expanding Gaza 'yellow line' to derail ceasefire talks, says Hamas

perspectives.keyFraming: A fragile US-brokered ceasefire holds on paper while strikes and stalled withdrawal talks continue.
perspectives.summary: AP (via the Philadelphia Inquirer) reported the toll topping 73,000 with Israeli strikes continuing despite the ceasefire and no agreement yet on the next phase of withdrawal.

sources.title: Palestinian death toll in Gaza tops 73,000 as Israel launches strikes despite ceasefire

stories.headline: G7 opens in Évian under shadow of US-Iran deal, Hormuz reopening, Ukraine and trade strains
stories.summary: The G7 summit opened June 15 in Évian-les-Bains, France, dominated by the confirmed US-Iran deal, the Ukraine war, AI governance and trade. The UK, France, Germany and Italy welcomed the agreement as 'a moment of opportunity,' urging rapid implementation and 'unrestricted freedom of navigation' through the Strait of Hormuz; Europe signaled sanctions calibration. Zelensky is set to join a Ukraine session but will not hold a one-on-one with Trump.

perspectives.keyFraming: A G7 dominated by scrutiny of Trump's Iran deal, with Europe seeking to shape implementation amid friction with Washington.
perspectives.summary: France 24 and CNBC report the summit convening as the Iran deal was confirmed, with items spanning Hormuz reopening, Lebanon, Ukraine air defense, tariffs and a rare-earth supply crisis; Zelensky attends a dedicated Ukraine session without a Trump bilateral.

sources.title: G7 summit opens in France with Ukraine war, new US-Iran agreement on agenda
World leaders welcome U.S.-Iran deal as Europe signals sanctions relief, urges Hormuz reopening

perspectives.keyFraming: France welcomes the deal and centers the summit on implementation, Hormuz, Lebanon and a final nuclear accord.
perspectives.summary: Host Macron welcomed the US-Iran agreement and called for rapid, complete implementation by all belligerents, naming Hormuz's lasting reopening and support for Lebanon as summit aims.

sources.title: G7 leaders meet in France after US and Iran declare agreement to end war
